prompt:
Growing the notification center (notifications.ts) for the bell-icon redesign. Three pieces, acceptance in test_read_group_retention.ts:

Read state. Every notification carries read: false from birth. markRead(id) returns true only when it actually flipped something (unknown id or already-read -> false), markAllRead() returns how many it flipped, unreadCount() for the badge, and list accepts an optional { unreadOnly: true } filter that keeps the usual newest-first order.

Grouping. grouped() -> [{ kind, count, latestAt, items }] — one bucket per kind, buckets ordered by their most recent notification (latestAt), items inside newest-first like list().

Retention. setRetention({ maxAgeMs?, maxCount?, keepUnread? }) plus an explicit prune() that returns the number removed; publish() must also prune automatically so the store can't grow unbounded. Age rule: strictly older than maxAgeMs goes (exactly maxAgeMs old stays); keepUnread: true exempts unread items from the AGE rule only. maxCount is a hard cap applied after the age pass — oldest out first, read or not, no exemptions. No policy configured means prune() does nothing.

The clock stays injectable like it is now — the suite runs on a fake clock, zero sleeps. test_notifications.ts pins current behavior; don't touch either test file. Green means: node --test test_notifications.ts test_read_group_retention.ts
